title: Caythorpe Station Lancaster Memorial
crew_summary: 6 killed; pilot Plt Off. Richard Albert George Miller

external_narratives.summary: IBCC feature on Lancaster PB812 AR-Y of 460 Squadron RAAF. Six crew killed on 10 February 1945 during a daytime navigation training flight from Binbrook. The aircraft was seen diving vertically over Caythorpe with flames from both wings.
key_details: All crew RAAF except RAF flight engineer
No operations that day, crew on training sortie
Bomb aimer Arnold Kloeden absent due to illness, survived

external_narratives.summary: Memorial record for Lancaster PB812. Two plaques unveiled on 10 September 2016: one at St Vincent's Church and one on Love Lane near the crash site, organised by Linda Pope, niece of crew member Rhod Pope.
key_details: Organised by Rhod Pope's niece Linda Pope
Two plaques: church and crash site on Love Lane

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museums register entry for the crew of Lancaster PB812 memorial plaque at Caythorpe Station, Lincolnshire.
International memorial database entry for the Lancaster PB812 crash memorial at Caythorpe, documenting the crash location and crew details.
